rows.requirement: Create Checkout initiates a new checkout session via POST /checkout-sessions
rows.quote: | [Create Checkout](checkout.md#create-checkout)     | `POST` | `/checkout-sessions`               | Create a checkout session. |
rows.notes: Operation/verb/endpoint binding from the REST operations table; keyword normalized to MUST. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_create_checkout asserts 200/201 with a checkout id.

rows.requirement: Get Checkout retrieves current state via GET /checkout-sessions/{id}
rows.quote: | [Get Checkout](checkout.md#get-checkout)           | `GET`  | `/checkout-sessions/{id}`          | Get a checkout session.    |
rows.notes: Verb/endpoint binding. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_get_checkout asserts 200 and returns the same id.

rows.requirement: Update Checkout updates a session via PUT /checkout-sessions/{id}
rows.quote: | [Update Checkout](checkout.md#update-checkout)     | `PUT`  | `/checkout-sessions/{id}`          | Update a checkout session. |
rows.notes: Verb=PUT enforces full-replacement semantics. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_update_checkout asserts 200.

rows.requirement: Complete Checkout places the order via POST /checkout-sessions/{id}/complete
rows.quote: | [Complete Checkout](checkout.md#complete-checkout) | `POST` | `/checkout-sessions/{id}/complete` | Place the order.           |
rows.notes: Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_complete_checkout asserts 200, status 'completed', and order present.

rows.requirement: Cancel Checkout cancels a session via POST /checkout-sessions/{id}/cancel
rows.quote: | [Cancel Checkout](checkout.md#cancel-checkout)     | `POST` | `/checkout-sessions/{id}/cancel`   | Cancel a checkout session. |
rows.notes: Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_cancel_checkout asserts 200 and status 'canceled'.

rows.requirement: Update Checkout performs a full replacement of the checkout resource; the platform is REQUIRED to send the entire checkout resource
rows.quote:
Performs a full replacement of the checkout resource.
The platform is **REQUIRED** to send the entire checkout resource containing any
data updates to write-only data fields. The resource provided in the request
will replace the existing checkout session state on the business side.
rows.notes: REQUIRED normalized to MUST. Full-replacement (PUT) semantics are behavioral on the receiver; a dropped field being lost is only observable against a stateful receiver, not via base schema. Restated in checkout-rest.md L172-173 'Each PUT replaces the entire session'.

rows.requirement: Each PUT replaces the entire session, so clients must include all previously set fields they wish to retain
rows.quote:
Each PUT replaces the entire session,
so clients must include all previously set fields they wish to retain.
rows.notes: Lower-case 'must' in prose; normative. Reinforces CHK-006 full-replacement semantics.

rows.requirement: Complete Checkout response is the checkout object with the order field populated (order provides id and permalink_url)
rows.quote:
The response of this
call is the checkout object with the `order` field populated in it. The returned
`order` provides necessary identifiers, such as `id` and `permalink_url`,
that can be used to reference the full state of the placed order.
rows.notes: Descriptive 'is' normalized to MUST. order is not in base required[]; presence-on-complete is conditional, so schema_enforced=false. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_complete_checkout asserts order, order.id, order.permalink_url present.

rows.requirement: At order persistence, fields from Checkout MAY be used to construct the order representation
rows.quote:
At the time of order persistence, fields from `Checkout` **MAY** be used
to construct the order representation (i.e. information like `line_items`,
`fulfillment` will be used to create the initial order representation).
rows.notes: Permissive.

rows.requirement: If a checkout session cannot be canceled (already canceled or completed), businesses SHOULD send back an error indicating the operation is not allowed
rows.quote:
If the checkout session cannot be canceled (e.g. checkout session is
already canceled or completed), then businesses **SHOULD** send back an error
indicating the operation is not allowed.
rows.notes: Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_cancel_is_idempotent / test_cannot_cancel_completed_checkout assert non-200 (the suite treats this as MUST-not-200, stricter than the spec's SHOULD).

rows.requirement: Any checkout session with a status that is not equal to completed or canceled SHOULD be cancelable
rows.quote:
Any checkout session with a status
that is not equal to `completed` or `canceled` **SHOULD** be cancelable.
rows.notes: Defines completed and canceled as terminal/non-cancelable states. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_cancel_checkout cancels a non-terminal session successfully.

rows.requirement: After a checkout session reaches the state completed it is considered immutable
rows.quote:
* After a checkout session reaches the state "completed", it is considered
    immutable.
rows.notes: Stated descriptively (no MUST keyword) but normative; keyword normalized to MUST. State-transition legality is prose-only, not schema-enforced. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_cannot_update_completed_checkout / test_cannot_cancel_completed_checkout / test_complete_is_idempotent assert non-200 on mutating a completed checkout.

rows.requirement: Status field enum is constrained to the six lifecycle values
rows.quote:
"enum": [
        "incomplete",
        "requires_escalation",
        "ready_for_complete",
        "complete_in_progress",
        "completed",
        "canceled"
      ],
rows.notes: Schema enum enforces status values. Identical six-value enum to 2026-04-08. Terminal states completed/canceled per lifecycle diagram (checkout.md L86-93). Oracle: lifecycle tests assert status 'canceled'/'completed'.

rows.requirement: Checkout response MUST include required top-level fields: ucp, id, line_items, status, currency, totals, links
rows.quote:
"required": [
    "ucp",
    "id",
    "line_items",
    "status",
    "currency",
    "totals",
    "links"
  ],
rows.notes: Schema required[] on the response object; identical set to 2026-04-08. These are response-required; on request bodies several are ucp_request:omit (see CHK-016..020). links is mandatory for legal compliance.

rows.requirement: Checkout status indicates the current phase and required action; the business sets the status
rows.quote:
The checkout `status` field indicates the current phase of the session and
determines what action is required next. The business sets the status; the
platform receives messages indicating what's needed to progress.
rows.notes: Defines status semantics and authority (business-set). Keyword normalized to MUST from descriptive prose.

rows.requirement: Top-level checkout id is omitted on create and complete requests and required on update requests (ucp_request)
rows.notes: Per-operation request requiredness via the ucp_request annotation on id; NOTE 2026-01-23 differs from 2026-04-08 where top-level id is a flat ucp_request:'omit'. Here id is required on update (id-in-URL plus body). ucp_request is a UCP annotation, not standard JSON-Schema, so a plain validator would not enforce it; schema_enforced=false.

rows.requirement: ucp envelope field is omitted on request bodies (ucp_request: omit)
rows.quote:
"$ref": "../ucp.json#/$defs/response_checkout_schema",
      "ucp_request": "omit"
rows.notes: Response-only field; ucp_request annotation, not base-schema-enforced. RECLASSIFIED manual->testable (2026-07-03): the OFFICIAL resolver arbitrates ucp_request annotations — the ucp property MUST be absent from every request-op resolution and present on the response resolution (RootCheck, schema_check_01_11_01_23.py).

rows.requirement: line_items is required on create and update requests and omitted on complete requests
rows.notes: Per-operation request requiredness via ucp_request; not enforced by a plain validator against base checkout.json (only the response required[] is). Needs a UCP request-profile validator.

rows.requirement: currency is omitted on create, update, and complete requests (merchant-determined, response-only)
rows.notes: currency is response-required (in required[]) but omitted on all request operations: the merchant determines currency from address/context/geo IP. In 2026-04-08 currency carries a flat ucp_request:'omit'; here it is the explicit per-operation create/update/complete omit form, equivalent effect. ucp_request annotation, not base-schema-enforced. RECLASSIFIED manual->testable (2026-07-03): resolver-arbitrated like CHK-017 (currency absent from all request-op resolutions, present on response; RootCheck, schema_check_01_11_01_23.py).

rows.requirement: status, totals, messages, links, expires_at, continue_url, and order are response-only (ucp_request: omit) and omitted on requests
rows.quote:
"description": "Checkout state indicating the current phase and required action. See Checkout Status lifecycle documentation for state transition details.",
      "ucp_request": "omit"
rows.notes: Covers status(L74), totals(L91), messages(L99), links(L107), expires_at(L113), continue_url(L119), order(L132) — all ucp_request:omit. Annotation, not base-schema-enforced. RECLASSIFIED manual->testable (2026-07-03): resolver-arbitrated like CHK-017 (one RootCheck per response-only property; schema_check_01_11_01_23.py).

rows.requirement: payment is optional on create and update, and required on complete requests
rows.notes: Payment optional on create (matches prose L42-45) and update; required on complete. Per-operation requiredness via ucp_request, not base-schema-enforced. Oracle (CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_complete_checkout) sends a payment payload on complete but does not assert rejection-on-missing.

rows.requirement: The payment object is optional on checkout creation and may be omitted for use cases that don't require payment processing
rows.quote:
The `payment` object is optional on checkout creation and may be omitted for
use cases that don't require payment processing (e.g., quote generation, cart
management).
rows.notes: Prose restatement of CHK-021 create-optionality.

rows.requirement: buyer is optional on create/update and omitted on complete requests
rows.notes: Applies to buyer(L48) and context(L56). All buyer fields optional, enabling progressive build (checkout-rest.md L173-175). NOTE: 2026-01-23 base checkout.json has a context field (and NO signals/attribution fields, unlike 2026-04-08).

rows.requirement: context is response-omitted (ucp_response: omit) — provisional buyer hints supplied only on requests
rows.quote: "ucp_response": "omit"
rows.notes: context is request-only (optional on create/update; ucp_response:omit). Businesses SHOULD use these provisional hints when authoritative data is absent (checkout.md L369-372). 2026-01-23 has context where 2026-04-08 had signals/attribution.

rows.requirement: Businesses MUST provide continue_url when returning status = requires_escalation (Availability)
rows.quote:
Businesses **MUST** provide `continue_url` when returning `status` =
`requires_escalation`.
rows.notes: Conditional invariant tied to runtime status; JSON Schema cannot enforce conditional presence. The schema description on continue_url notes it but does not encode the conditional. Requires a receiver that emits requires_escalation.

rows.requirement: For non-terminal statuses other than requires_escalation, businesses SHOULD provide continue_url
rows.quote:
For all other non-terminal statuses (`incomplete`,
`ready_for_complete`, `complete_in_progress`), businesses **SHOULD** provide
`continue_url`.
rows.notes: SHOULD; prose-only.

rows.requirement: For terminal states (completed, canceled), continue_url SHOULD be omitted
rows.quote:
For terminal states (`completed`, `canceled`), `continue_url`
**SHOULD** be omitted.
rows.notes: SHOULD; prose-only. Reaffirms completed/canceled as the two terminal states.

rows.requirement: continue_url MUST be an absolute HTTPS URL
rows.quote:
The `continue_url` **MUST** be an absolute HTTPS URL and **SHOULD** preserve
checkout state for seamless handoff.
rows.notes: Schema declares continue_url format:uri but does not enforce absolute-HTTPS-only; format:uri also accepts http and relative refs in most validators, so schema_enforced=false. Needs a receiver returning continue_url.

rows.requirement: continue_url SHOULD preserve checkout state for seamless handoff
rows.quote:
The `continue_url` **MUST** be an absolute HTTPS URL and **SHOULD** preserve
checkout state for seamless handoff.
rows.notes: State-preservation is opaque to the platform; not observable as a wire constraint.

rows.requirement: Businesses MAY implement continue_url state preservation using either server-side state or checkout permalink
rows.quote:
Businesses **MAY** implement state
preservation using either approach:
rows.notes: Permissive option.

rows.requirement: Businesses SHOULD implement support for the checkout permalink (stateless) format
rows.quote:
Businesses **SHOULD** implement support for
this format to facilitate checkout handoff and accelerated entry—for example, a
platform can prefill checkout state when initiating a buy-now flow.
rows.notes: Permalink is a REST-specific construct.

rows.requirement: Platform MUST use continue_url when checkout status is requires_escalation (Platform guideline)
rows.quote: * **MUST** use `continue_url` when checkout status is `requires_escalation`.
rows.notes: Platform-side behavior; not a wire shape testable on the business response.

rows.requirement: Platform MAY use continue_url to hand off to business UI in other situations
rows.quote: * **MAY** use `continue_url` to hand off to business UI in other situations.
rows.notes: Platform-side permissive.

rows.requirement: When performing handoff, platform SHOULD prefer business-provided continue_url over platform-constructed permalinks
rows.quote:
* When performing handoff, **SHOULD** prefer business-provided
    `continue_url` over platform-constructed checkout permalinks.
rows.notes: Platform-side behavior.

rows.requirement: Business MUST send a confirmation email after the checkout has been completed
rows.quote: * **MUST** send a confirmation email after the checkout has been completed.
rows.notes: Side effect outside the HTTP response; not observable via the API surface.

rows.requirement: Business SHOULD provide accurate error messages
rows.quote: * **SHOULD** provide accurate error messages.
rows.notes: Subjective quality requirement.

rows.requirement: Logic handling the checkout sessions MUST be deterministic
rows.quote: * Logic handling the checkout sessions **MUST** be deterministic.
rows.notes: Determinism across repeated calls is only weakly observable; a partial check would require replaying identical requests. No single-response oracle.

rows.requirement: Business MUST provide continue_url when returning status = requires_escalation (Business guideline restatement)
rows.quote:
* **MUST** provide `continue_url` when returning `status` =
    `requires_escalation`.
rows.notes: Duplicate of CHK-025 under Guidelines; kept for coverage.

rows.requirement: Business MUST include at least one message with severity: escalation when returning status = requires_escalation
rows.quote:
* **MUST** include at least one message with `severity: escalation` when
    returning `status` = `requires_escalation`.
rows.notes: VERSION DELTA: 2026-01-23 requires a message with severity 'escalation'; 2026-04-08 instead requires severity of 'requires_buyer_input' or 'requires_buyer_review'. Cross-field conditional (status vs messages[].severity); not expressible in base checkout.json. Needs a receiver that emits requires_escalation.

rows.requirement: Business SHOULD provide continue_url in all non-terminal checkout responses
rows.quote: * **SHOULD** provide `continue_url` in all non-terminal checkout responses.
rows.notes: SHOULD; restates CHK-026.

rows.requirement: Platform SHOULD inspect messages array and attempt to resolve incomplete via Update Checkout
rows.quote:
**`incomplete`**: Checkout session is missing required information or has
    issues that need resolution. Platform should inspect `messages` array for
    context and should attempt to resolve via Update Checkout.
rows.notes: Platform-side behavior; status-value semantics for 'incomplete'.

rows.requirement: On requires_escalation, platform should inspect messages, resolve any recoverable errors first, then hand off to buyer via continue_url
rows.quote:
**`requires_escalation`**: Checkout session requires information that
    cannot be provided via API, or buyer input is required. Platform should
    inspect `messages` to understand what's needed (see Error Handling below).
    If any `recoverable` errors exist, resolve those first.
    Then hand off to buyer via `continue_url`.
rows.notes: Platform-side behavior; status-value semantics.

rows.requirement: On ready_for_complete the checkout has all necessary information and the platform can finalize programmatically and call Complete Checkout
rows.quote:
**`ready_for_complete`**: Checkout session has all necessary information
    and platform can finalize programmatically. Platform can call
    Complete Checkout.
rows.notes: Defines the only status from which autonomous Complete is permitted (lifecycle diagram L77-89: Complete edge originates at ready_for_complete). Keyword 'can' normalized to MAY. Permission, not enforceable on a single response.

rows.requirement: complete_in_progress means the business is processing the Complete Checkout request
rows.quote:
**`complete_in_progress`**: Business is processing the Complete Checkout
    request.
rows.notes: Status-value semantics; non-terminal transitional state. Descriptive, no RFC2119 keyword.

rows.requirement: completed means the order was placed successfully
rows.quote: **`completed`**: Order placed successfully.
rows.notes: Terminal-state semantics. Oracle: CheckoutLifecycleTest.test_complete_checkout asserts final status 'completed'. Descriptive, no RFC2119 keyword.

rows.requirement: On canceled (session invalid or expired), platform should start a new checkout session if needed
rows.quote:
**`canceled`**: Checkout session is invalid or expired. Platform should
    start a new checkout session if needed.
rows.notes: Platform-side behavior; defines canceled as terminal (can occur from any state per diagram L91-93).

rows.requirement: Businesses SHOULD surface requires_buyer_input/review messages as early as possible; platforms SHOULD prioritize resolving recoverable errors before initiating handoff
rows.quote:
Businesses **SHOULD** surface such messages as early as possible, and platforms
**SHOULD** prioritize resolving recoverable errors before initiating handoff.
rows.notes: Two SHOULDs (business + platform) about error-stack ordering.

rows.requirement: Get Checkout: after cancellation or completion it is up to the business what to return; the platform has no specific enforcement for a TTL
rows.quote:
After cancellation or
completion it is up to the business on what to return (i.e this can be a long
lived state or expire after a particular TTL - resulting in a 'not found'
error). From the platform there is no specific enforcement for a TTL of the
checkout.
rows.notes: Business discretion on post-terminal Get behavior; expires_at default TTL 6h (schema L112).

rows.requirement: Product data provided by the business through feeds SHOULD match the actual attributes returned in the create response
rows.quote:
product data (price/title etc.) provided by the business through the feeds
**SHOULD** match the actual attributes returned in the response.
rows.notes: Cross-system consistency recommendation.

rows.requirement: All REST request and response bodies MUST be valid JSON per RFC 8259
rows.quote:
All request and response bodies **MUST** be valid JSON as specified in
[RFC 8259](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8259){ target="_blank" }.
rows.notes: JSON well-formedness is a parse-level check, upstream of schema validation.

rows.requirement: All REST endpoints MUST be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version 1.3
rows.quote:
All REST endpoints **MUST** be served over HTTPS with minimum TLS version
1.3.
rows.notes: Transport-layer check; not observable in JSON body. browser_capable=false (TLS-version inspection not available from browser fetch).

rows.requirement: All requests MUST include the UCP-Agent header containing the platform profile URI (RFC 8941 dictionary syntax)
rows.quote:
* **UCP-Agent**: All requests **MUST** include the `UCP-Agent` header
    containing the platform profile URI using Dictionary Structured Field syntax
    ([RFC 8941](https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/rfc8941){target="_blank"}).
    Format: `profile="https://platform.example/profile"`.
rows.notes: Request-side header requirement on the platform; checked on requests, not on the checkout response body. Reclassified 2026-07-03 (adversarial review F2, suite-wide policy): 'All requests MUST include the UCP-Agent header' binds the REQUEST AUTHOR (platform) — the spec mandates no business-side rejection of header-less requests, so a merchant check grading a 4xx is a contrapositive stretch that can false-deviate a tolerant conformant merchant (same subject-binding as ORD-015/DISC-006, decided the same way for consistency). The suite's own probe traffic satisfies the MUST. Candidate for a client-bound exemption once version-scoped exemptions land.

rows.requirement: Operations that modify state SHOULD support idempotency (via Idempotency-Key)
rows.quote:
* **Idempotency-Key**: Operations that modify state **SHOULD** support
    idempotency. When provided, the server **MUST**:
rows.notes: State-modifying ops (create/update/complete/cancel) should accept Idempotency-Key. Oracle: IdempotencyTest.test_idempotency_create / test_idempotency_update / test_idempotency_complete / test_idempotency_cancel exercise the key across all four mutating operations.

rows.requirement: When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST store the key with the operation result for at least 24 hours
rows.quote: 1. Store the key with the operation result for at least 24 hours.
rows.notes: 24-hour retention window is not directly assertable in the test suite (no time-travel); the official tests only validate same-session duplicate behavior. needs-receiver.

rows.requirement: When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST return the cached result for duplicate keys
rows.quote: 2. Return the cached result for duplicate keys.
rows.notes: Oracle: IdempotencyTest.test_idempotency_create / test_idempotency_update / test_idempotency_complete / test_idempotency_cancel send the same request twice with the same key and assert response2.status_code == response1.status_code and response2.json() == response1.json() (identical cached body).

rows.requirement: When an Idempotency-Key is provided, the server MUST return 409 Conflict if the key is reused with different parameters
rows.quote: 3. Return `409 Conflict` if the key is reused with different parameters.
rows.notes: Oracle: IdempotencyTest.test_idempotency_create reuses the key with currency='EUR' and asserts 409; test_idempotency_update reuses with a changed line_items quantity and asserts 409; test_idempotency_complete reuses with a changed credential token and asserts 409. (test_idempotency_cancel has no body, so no conflict case.)

rows.requirement: 409 Conflict indicates the request could not be completed due to a conflict (e.g., idempotent key reuse)
rows.quote: | `409 Conflict`              | The request could not be completed due to a conflict (e.g., idempotent key reuse). |
rows.notes: Defines the 409 status semantics referenced by the Idempotency-Key rules (IDM-004) and by terminal-state conflicts. Keyword normalized to MUST from the status-code table. Oracle: IdempotencyTest 409 assertions; also CheckoutLifecycleTest terminal-state non-200 assertions.

notes: Spec version 2026-01-23, pinned commit dcf7eac71fc370dcc8768fcdbc5aa737037cca05. Sources read: docs/specification/checkout.md, docs/specification/checkout-rest.md, source/schemas/shopping/checkout.json (vendored under conformance/.vendor/ucp-2026-01-23/). Schema citations use ucp:source/schemas/shopping/checkout.json to match the vendored path; verify_register resolves the 'ucp' prefix for version 2026-01-23 to the ucp-2026-01-23 tree.

The vendored markdown and schema carry literal capability/example version '2026-01-11' (checkout.md L20; checkout.json L5; example payloads throughout checkout-rest.md). This is the capability version label, not the spec release; the prompt pins this extraction to spec 2026-01-23, so all rows are tagged ['2026-01-23'].

VERSION DELTAS 2026-01-23 vs 2026-04-08: (1) Status enum, response required[] (ucp,id,line_items,status,currency,totals,links), and currency-as-merchant-determined are IDENTICAL across both. (2) Escalation message rule differs: 2026-01-23 requires a message with `severity: escalation` (CHK-039, checkout.md L270); 2026-04-08 requires severity 'requires_buyer_input' or 'requires_buyer_review'. (3) Base checkout.json request fields differ: 2026-01-23 has a `context` field (request-only, ucp_response:omit) and NO signals/attribution fields; 2026-04-08 carries signals/attribution. (4) Top-level `id` ucp_request differs: 2026-01-23 uses the per-operation form {create:omit, update:required, complete:omit} (CHK-016); 2026-04-08 uses a flat ucp_request:'omit'. (5) `currency` in 2026-01-23 uses the explicit per-operation omit form {create:omit, update:omit, complete:omit} (CHK-019) vs the flat 'omit' in 2026-04-08 (equivalent effect).

official_oracle=true rows are backed by conformance/.vendor/conformance/checkout_lifecycle_test.py and idempotency_test.py. Lifecycle oracles: test_create_checkout (CHK-001), test_get_checkout (CHK-002), test_update_checkout (CHK-003), test_complete_checkout (CHK-004, CHK-008, CHK-045), test_cancel_checkout (CHK-005, CHK-011), test_cancel_is_idempotent + test_cannot_cancel_completed_checkout (CHK-010), test_cannot_update_completed_checkout + test_cannot_cancel_completed_checkout + test_complete_is_idempotent (CHK-012), status-enum assertions (CHK-013). Idempotency oracles: test_idempotency_{create,update,complete,cancel} (IDM-001, IDM-003), 409-on-different-body in create/update/complete (IDM-004, IDM-005).

schema_enforced=true ONLY for genuine JSON-Schema constructs on checkout.json: status enum (CHK-013, L65) and response required[] (CHK-014, L9). All `ucp_request` / `ucp_response` annotations (omit/required/optional per operation) are UCP-specific, NOT standard JSON-Schema keywords — a vanilla draft 2020-12 validator against base checkout.json would not enforce them, hence schema_enforced=false for CHK-016..024 (a UCP-aware request-profile validator would catch these).

continue_url MUST-be-absolute-HTTPS (CHK-028) is schema_enforced=false: the schema only declares format:'uri', which most validators treat as annotation-only and which permits http/relative; the absolute-HTTPS constraint is prose-only.

State-transition legality and terminal-state immutability (CHK-012) are prose invariants, not schema-encoded — base checkout.json has no if/then or transition modeling. Their oracle is behavioral (HTTP non-200 on an illegal transition). Lifecycle terminal states are completed and canceled (diagram checkout.md L86-93 and status-value definitions); incomplete, requires_escalation, ready_for_complete, complete_in_progress are non-terminal. Autonomous Complete is permitted only from ready_for_complete (CHK-043).

Idempotency: the three-step server MUST (store >=24h, return cached on duplicate, 409 on same-key-different-body) is quoted verbatim from checkout-rest.md L1216-1219. IDM-002 (24h retention) is needs-receiver (not time-assertable by the suite); IDM-003/IDM-004 are directly asserted by IdempotencyTest. test_idempotency_cancel has no request body, so it validates only the duplicate-returns-cached path, not the 409-conflict path.

Several requirements are platform-side behaviors (CHK-032/033/034/041/042/046) or out-of-band side effects (CHK-035 confirmation email) that cannot be validated from a single business HTTP response; marked manual or needs-receiver accordingly.
